# Sgraal — Memory Governance Protocol for AI Agents

> Before an AI agent acts, Sgraal checks if its memory is still true.

## What is Sgraal?

Sgraal is a memory governance protocol that provides a preflight scoring API for AI agents. It evaluates whether the memory an agent is relying on is fresh, consistent, and trustworthy — returning a risk score (Ω_MEM) and a recommended action in under 10ms.

## API Endpoint

POST https://api.sgraal.com/v1/preflight

Accepts memory entries with trust, conflict, age, and belief metadata. Returns:
- omega_mem_final: Risk score (0-100)
- recommended_action: USE_MEMORY | WARN | ASK_USER | BLOCK
- assurance_score: Confidence score
- component_breakdown: 9 individual risk components

## Install

- Python: pip install sgraal
- Node.js: npm install @sgraal/mcp
- REST API: No SDK needed, call directly with curl or any HTTP client
- Claude Desktop: MCP server via npx @sgraal/mcp

## Scoring Components

The Ω_MEM formula uses 9 weighted risk components:
1. s_freshness — how old is the memory
2. s_drift — has the memory drifted from its original meaning
3. s_provenance — how trusted is the source
4. s_propagation — blast radius if memory is wrong
5. r_recall — likelihood of accurate recall
6. r_encode — quality of original encoding
7. s_interference — conflict between sources (Dempster-Shafer K)
8. s_recovery — ability to recover from memory errors
9. r_belief — model belief divergence

## Memory Types

episodic, semantic, preference, tool_state, shared_workflow, policy, identity

## Pricing

Free: 10,000 calls/month
Usage-based: $0.001 per call after free tier

- Website: https://sgraal.com
